summary: FiveM game cheat with Microsoft-impersonating persistence, obfuscated remote-exec payload, and HWID exfiltration
details: Package ships as a 'Sensivity Control Panel' but installs a FiveM cheat loader. On loading launcher.js (declared as both `main` and `bin`), the code writes an HKCU Run-key entry named `OneDriveUpdate` pointing at a bundled `OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs` that silently re-launches `node launcher.js` at every login — Microsoft-impersonating persistence on the installer's machine. launcher.js then executes a 172 KB obfuscator.io-protected blob via `eval(fs.readFileSync('server.obf.js'))`; the decoded code generates a hardware fingerprint (volume serial + Windows MachineGuid + hostname, SHA-256), POSTs `{key, hwid, nonce, app:'forte', version:'2.5'}` to a hardcoded license endpoint, opens a socket.io channel that dispatches commands into the bundled 6.8 MB native addon `sens.node` (whose strings include `Teleport`, `LeftFoot`, `LeftHand`, `Head`, `__licenseAccepted` — FiveM aimbot/teleport surface), and includes a `runcode` resource-execution path gated by a hardcoded RSA public key. The CLI's `sens update` subcommand runs `taskkill /F /IM node.exe` and `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe` against every Node and PowerShell process on the host, then reinstalls itself globally via `npm i -g sensivity@latest` (unpinned mutable target, guaranteeing future malicious versions auto-deploy). Combined: opaque obfuscated code execution on load, persistence with Microsoft impersonation, hardware-fingerprint exfiltration to an attacker-controlled host, an undocumented native binary with game-cheat capability, and host-process termination plus unpinned self-update.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
